chunks.text: Research Area: SP | EECS at UC Berkeley. Signal Processing (SP). Overview. Topics: Theory and Algorithms. Adaptive signal processing, machine learning, and signal modeling; indexing, searching, and retrieval; Multirate and multi-channel processing; Restoration and enhancement; Signal analysis, identification, spectral estimation, and understanding; Signal representation, compression, coding, quantization and sampling; Statistical signal processing, detection, estimation, and classification; Watermarking, encryption, and data hiding; Wavelets, filter banks, time frequency techniques.
chunks.question: What are the main topics in Signal Processing theory and algorithms at UC Berkeley?
chunks.answer: Adaptive processing, machine learning, and signal modeling.

chunks.text: Signal Processing Applications. Audio, speech, image, and video processing; graphics; Biological & biomedical signals; Computer vision; Radar and lidar; Geophysical signals; Synthetic signals; Astronomical signals.
chunks.question: What are some applications of Signal Processing at UC Berkeley?
chunks.answer: Audio, video, biomedical signals, and computer vision.

chunks.text: Signal Processing Systems. VLSI architectures; Embedded and real-time software; Capture, acquisition, and sensing; Sensor networks; Imaging; Auditory enhancement. Research Centers. Berkeley Center for Computational Imaging. Berkeley Laboratory for Information and System Sciences. Video and Image Processing Lab.
chunks.question: What systems and research centers are involved in Signal Processing at UC Berkeley?
chunks.answer: VLSI architectures, sensor networks, and computational imaging centers.

chunks.text: Faculty. Primary: Gopala Krishna Anumanchipalli, Steven Conolly, Jiantao Jiao, Chunlei Liu, Michael Lustig, Song Mei, Kannan Ramchandran (coordinator), Benjamin Recht, Anant Sahai, Laura Waller, Bin Yu, Avideh Zakhor. Secondary: Venkat Anantharam, Babak Ayazifar, Brian A. Barsky, Peter Bartlett, Thomas F. Budinger, Laurent El Ghaoui, Michael Jordan, Preeya Khanna, Edward A. Lee, Jitendra Malik, David G. Messerschmitt, Nelson Morgan, Aditya Varma Muppala, Ren Ng, Ali Niknejad, Jan M. Rabaey, Gireeja Ranade, S. Shankar Sastry, Stella Yu.
chunks.question: Who are the primary and secondary faculty in Signal Processing at UC Berkeley?
chunks.answer: Primary includes Anumanchipalli, Lustig, and Ramchandran.

chunks.text: Faculty Awards. National Academy of Sciences (NAS) Member: Jitendra Malik, 2015. Bin Yu, 2014. Michael Jordan, 2010. National Academy of Engineering (NAE) Member: Jitendra Malik, 2011. Michael Jordan, 2010. S. Shankar Sastry, 2001. Thomas F. Budinger, 1996. David G. Messerschmitt, 1990. American Academy of Arts and Sciences Member: Jitendra Malik, 2013. Bin Yu, 2013. Michael Jordan, 2010. S. Shankar Sastry, 2003. Berkeley Citation: S. Shankar Sastry, 2018. Edward A. Lee, 2018. David G. Messerschmitt, 2005. Thomas F. Budinger, 2004. Sloan Research Fellow: Song Mei, 2025. Preeya Khanna, 2024. Ren Ng, 2017. Michael Lustig, 2013. Benjamin Recht, 2011.
chunks.question: What awards have Signal Processing faculty at UC Berkeley received?
chunks.answer: NAS, NAE, Arts and Sciences, and Sloan awards.

chunks.text: Related Courses. EECS 126. Probability and Random Processes. EE 120. Signals and Systems. EE 123. Digital Signal Processing. EE 126. Probability and Random Processes. EE C145B. Medical Imaging Signals and Systems. EE 221A. Linear System Theory. EE C225E. Principles of Magnetic Resonance Imaging. EE 290T. Advanced Topics in Electrical Engineering: Advanced Topics in Signal Processing.
chunks.question: What are some related courses for Signal Processing at UC Berkeley?
